step_by_step_thinking:
Based on the previously discussed rationale, I concur with the decision to exclude option B (Pulmonary artery embolization). This procedure involves blocking major pulmonary arteries which do not have the necessary precision to target the specific bronchial arteries causing hemoptysis. Instead, procedures such as bronchial artery embolization provide more direct and effective means to manage and stop hemorrhaging within the lungs' airway system.

The final answer therefore stands firm with Option B being excluded.
